text:
Once upon a time in the magical land of Everwood, there was a small fairy named Frolio who lived in a hollowed-out ancient oak tree. Frolio loved to watch the world grow; flowers bloomed in spring, mushrooms sprang forth from the earth in summer, and the stars twinkled gently above in autumn.

One sunny day, while lazily dancing about gathering glittering sequins from fallen star dust, Frolio heard a peculiar noise. It was nothing strange—the wind rustling through the leaves—but something about it set Frolio's tiny, pixie-like nose twitching.

Curiosity piqued, Frolio fluttered down to investigate. To her astonishment, a group of giant birds with human mouths was sitting on the oak tree. They spoke in whispers and cracked their beaks open and closed.

"What are you doing here?" asked the lead bird in a deep voice.

"I'm just exploring," Frolio replied, flitting closer. The birds chuckled nervously
